rationale: The citation is used in a specific theoretical step, not just as a generic background mention. In discussing why loyalty intentions should mediate the satisfaction-shareholder value link, the authors cite Gupta, Lehmann, and Stuart (2004) as evidence connecting sales-related customer behavior to customer equity/CLV and firm value logic. This supports the conceptual setup of the citing paper's mediation framework, even though the citing study does not adopt the cited paper's valuation model directly.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Valuing Customers as conceptual support that customer-level economic outcomes (e.g., customer equity/CLV linked to sales behavior) can translate into firm value. It uses this premise to justify its satisfaction -> loyalty intentions -> shareholder value framing.
